18.30.sankara: That which knows the path of work and renunciation, what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, that intellect is Sattvic, O Arjuna.
18.30.meaning: Sattvic Intellect. Knowing the 'Difference' (Pravrittim cha nivrittim cha). Knowing 'Karyam' vs 'A-karyam' (Right vs Wrong). Knowing 'Bhayam' vs 'Abhayam' (Fear vs Courage).
18.30.application: Cultivate 'Discernment'. A Sattvic mind has a 'Clear Map'. It knows when to 'Step forward' and when to 'Withdraw'. It doesn't get 'Confused' by social pressure. It understands 'Bandham' (Bondage) and 'Moksha' (Freedom). Be a 'Map-Maker'.

18.31.sankara: That by which one wrongly understands Dharma and Adharma, and also what ought to be done and what ought not to be done, that intellect, O Arjuna, is Rajasic.
18.31.meaning: Rajasic Intellect. 'A-yathavat' (Invalid/Distorted) understanding. It sees 'Dharma' but through a 'Lens of Desire'. It's 'Confused' about 'Right and Wrong'.
18.31.application: Stop 'Rationalizing'. A Rajasic mind twists the 'Truth' to fit what it 'Wants'. It calls 'Greed' 'Ambition'. It calls 'Lust' 'Love'. If you are 'Always Justifying' your selfish choices, your 'Buddhi' is Rajasic. Clean the 'Lens' of your logic.

18.32.sankara: That which, enveloped in darkness, sees Adharma as Dharma and all things perverted, that intellect, O Arjuna, is Tamasic.
18.32.meaning: Tamasic Intellect. 'Adharmam dharmam iti manyate' (Thinking wrong is right). Covered in 'Tamas' (Darkness). Seeing 'Sarvārthān-viparītān-cha' (All things perverted/upside-down).
18.32.application: Escape the 'Upside-Down'. A Tamasic mind thinks 'Violence' is 'Strength' and 'Ignorance' is 'Bliss'. It is 'Totally Inverted'. If you find yourself 'Celebrating Evil' or 'Rejecting Good', your 'Software' is corrupted. Reboot with 'Simple Truths'.

18.33.sankara: The firmness by which one holds fast the functions of the mind, the life-force and the organs, through unswerving Yoga, that firmness, O Arjuna, is Sattvic.
18.33.meaning: Sattvic Firmness (Dhriti). 'A-vyabhicharinnya' (Unswerving). Controlling 1. Manas (Mind). 2. Prana (Life-force). 3. Indriya (Senses) through 'Yoga'.
18.33.application: Hold the 'Reins'. Sattvic willpower is the ability to say 'No' to a 'Distraction' and 'Yes' to the 'Self'. It's 'Yoga-dhriti'. It keeps your 'Energy' (Prana) and 'Thoughts' (Manas) on the 'Target'. Be 'Unswerving'.

18.34.sankara: But the firmness, O Arjuna, by which one holds fast to Dharma, desire and wealth, from attachment and from desire for reward, that firmness is Rajasic.
18.34.meaning: Rajasic Firmness. Holding onto 'Dharma, Kama, Artha' with 'Sangena' (Attachment) and 'Phala-akankshi' (Desire for results). It's 'Stubbornness for Gain'.
18.34.application: Don't be 'Obsessively Ambitious'. Rajasic willpower is when you are 'determined' only because of the 'Payoff'. You are 'Firm' as long as there is 'Money or Pleasure'. If the 'Reward' disappears, your 'Willpower' collapses. Build 'Internal Strength', not 'External Greed'.

18.35.sankara: That by which a stupid man does not abandon sleep, fear, grief, despair and also conceit, that firmness, O Arjuna, is Tamasic.
18.35.meaning: Tamasic Firmness. 'Duh-medha' (Foolish one) who holds onto 'Nidram' (Sleep), 'Bhayam' (Fear), 'Shokam' (Grief), and 'Mada' (Conceit). It's 'Willpower to stay Sad'.
18.35.application: Stop 'Self-Sabotage'. A Tamasic person is 'Determined' to stay in bed, 'Determined' to keep crying, and 'Determined' to be afraid. It takes 'Energy' to be 'Miserable'. Use that same 'Dhriti' to 'Let Go' instead. Don't be 'FIRM in your FEAR'.

18.36.sankara: Now hear from Me, O best of the Bharatas, of the threefold happiness, which one enjoys through practice, and in which one attains to the end of all pain.
18.36.meaning: The Triad of Joy. Krishna now analyzes 'Sukham' (Happiness). Even 'Joy' has Three Gunas. You can reach the 'End of Pain' (Duhkhantam) by choosing the right 'Flavor' of joy.
18.36.application: Audit your 'Pleasures'. Are you 'Happy' because you are 'Growing', or because you are 'Numbing'? Krishna is giving you the 'Internal Happiness Monitor'. Use it to reach the 'End of Sorrow' (Duhkhantam).

18.37.sankara: That which is like poison at first but like nectar in the end, which is born of the purity of one's own intellect, that happiness is declared to be Sattvic.
18.37.meaning: Sattvic Happiness. 'Visham-iva' (Like Poison) at start, 'Amrit-opamam' (Like Nectar) at end. Born of 'Atma-buddhi-prasada' (Serenity of self-intellect).
18.37.application: Pay the 'Upfront Cost'. Learning a skill, working out, or meditating feels like 'Poison' at first. It's 'Hard'. But the result is 'Permanent Nectar'. If it's 'Easy now', it might be 'Hard later'. If it's 'Hard now', it's 'Sattvic Joy'. Choose the 'Delayed Reward'.

18.38.sankara: That happiness which arises from the contact of the sense-organs with the objects, which is like nectar at first but in the end like poison, is declared to be Rajasic.
18.38.meaning: Rajasic Happiness. 'Vishaya-indriya-samyogat' (Contact of sense with object). 'Amrit-opamam' (Nectar) at start, 'Visham-iva' (Poison) at end.
18.38.application: Beware the 'Sugar Crash'. Junk food, scrolling social media, or brief lust feels great for 5 minutes (Nectar) but leaves you 'Empty and Sick' (Poison). It's 'Short-term Gain for Long-term Pain'. Stop 'Chasing the High'.

18.39.sankara: That happiness which both at first and as a consequence deludes the soul, arising from sleep, indolence and heedlessness, is declared to be Tamasic.
18.39.meaning: Tamasic Happiness. 'Moha-nam' (Deluding) at start AND end. Born of 'Nidra-alasya-pramada' (Sleep, laziness, and mistake/carelessness).
18.39.application: Don't confuse 'Sleep' with 'Deep Peace'. Tamasic joy is the 'Happiness' of checking out. It's 'Ignorance'. It 'Deludes' you into thinking you are okay while you are actually 'Stagnating'. Wake up to 'Sattvic Growth'.

18.40.sankara: There is no being on earth, or in heaven among the gods, that can be free from these three qualities born of Prakriti (matter).
18.40.meaning: The Universal Net. 'Na-tadasti' (There is nothing). Everything in the 'Created World' (Earth or Heaven) is 'Guna-mayi'. Everything is made of these three 'Threads'.
18.40.application: Accept the 'Hardware'. You can't 'Escape' the Gunas while you have a body/mind. But you can 'Manage' them. Don't fight the 'Net'; learn how to 'Untangle' the strands. 'Transcend' by 'Observing'. Everything is 'Prakriti' (Matter).

18.41.sankara: Of Brahmanas, Kshatriyas and Vaishyas, as also of Shudras, O Arjuna, the duties are distributed according to the qualities born of their own nature.
18.41.meaning: The Natural Division. Duties (Karmani) are distributed based on 'Svabhava-prabhavair gunaih' (Qualities born of one's own nature). It's 'Genetic' and 'Psychological'.
18.41.application: Find your 'Fit'. You aren't 'Equal' in your 'Function'. Some are born to 'Think', some to 'Lead', some to 'Trade', some to 'Serve'. Don't fight your 'Nature' (Svabhava); use it. 'Varna' is about 'Matching your Vibe' to your 'Work'.

18.42.sankara: Serenity, self-restraint, austerity, purity, forgiveness and also uprightness, knowledge, realization and belief in God are the duties of the Brahmanas, born of (their own) nature.
18.42.meaning: The Brahmin Nature (Intellectual/Spiritual). 1. Shamo (Peace). 2. Damo (Control). 3. Tapah (Simplicity). 4. Shaucham (Purity). 5. Jnanam-Vijnanam (Theoretical and Applied Knowledge).
18.42.application: If you are a 'Thinker', be 'Pure'. If your job is 'Knowledge', your 'Duty' is to be 'Peaceful' and 'Truthful'. You cannot be an 'Intellectual' and be 'Chaotic'. Master your 'Internal Environment' to illuminate the 'External' one.

18.43.sankara: Prowess, splendor, firmness, dexterity, and also, not fleeing from battle, generosity and lordliness are the duties of the Kshatriyas, born of (their own) nature.
18.43.meaning: The Kshatriya Nature (Leader/Protector). 1. Shauryam (Bravery). 2. Tejo (Brightness/Will). 3. Dhriti (Firmness). 4. Dakshyam (Resourcefulness). 5. Ishvara-bhavash-cha (Leadership).
18.43.application: If you are a 'Leader', be 'Brave'. Your 'Duty' is to 'Protect' and 'Support'. Don't 'Run' from 'Battles' (Life's challenges). A leader's job is not to 'Boss' people, but to 'Inspire' them through 'Fearlessness' and 'Generosity' (Danam).

18.44.sankara: Agriculture, cattle-rearing and trade are the duties of the Vaishyas, born of (their own) nature; and action consisting of service is the duty of the Shudras, born of (their own) nature.
18.44.meaning: The Economic and Support Nature. 1. Vaishya: Krishi (Agri), Gaurakshya (Animals), Vanijyam (Trade). 2. Shudra: Paricharyatmakam (Service/Support).
18.44.application: Be 'Essential'. Whether you 'Create Value' (Vaishya) or 'Enable Others' (Shudra), your work is 'Sacred'. A world needs 'Food and Trade' as much as 'Knowledge'. A world needs 'Support' as much as 'Leadership'. Do your part 'Excellently'.

18.45.sankara: Devoted each to his own duty, man attains perfection. How, engaged in his own duty, he attains perfection, that do thou hear.
18.45.meaning: The Path to Perfection. 'Swe swe karman-yabhiratah' (Intent on his own work). 'Siddhim vindati' (Attains perfection). You don't get 'Holy' by doing 'Another's Job'.
18.45.application: Focus on 'Your Desk'. You don't need to 'Go to the Himalayas' to find God. You find 'Perfection' by doing 'Your Specific Job' with 'Total Devotion'. Every profession is a 'Samskara' (Cleaning process) if done right.

18.46.sankara: He from whom is the evolution of all beings, by whom all this is pervaded, worshipping Him with his own duty, man attains perfection.
18.46.meaning: Work as Worship. Worshipping the 'Internal Source' (Yatah pravrittir bhutanam) through 'Sva-karmana' (One's own work).
18.46.application: The 'Sacred Office'. Your 'Spreadsheet', your 'Cooking', or your 'Cleaning' is your 'Puja' (Ritual). If you do it to 'Serve the Source' that pervades everything, your 'Work' is 'Worship'. God is in the 'Details' of your 'Daily Duty'.

18.47.sankara: Better is one's own duty, though devoid of merit, than the duty of another well performed. He who does the duty ordained by his own nature incurs no sin.
18.47.meaning: The Risk of Imitation. 'Shreyan sva-dharmo vishunah' (Better your own duty, even if flawed). Following 'Svabhava-niyatam' (Nature-ordained) work protects you from 'Dosham' (Sin/Entropy).
18.47.application: Be a 'First-rate You', not a 'Second-rate Someone Else'. Even if you are 'Average' at your own talent, it's better than being 'Great' at a 'Fake' role. Your 'Vocation' is your 'Vault'. Stay inside it; you are 'Safe' there.

18.48.sankara: One should not abandon, O Arjuna, the duty to which one is born, though faulty; for all undertakings are enveloped by evil, as fire by smoke.
18.48.meaning: The Imperfection of Action. 'Saha-jam karma' (Born duty) should not be abandoned even if it has 'Sadosham' (Faults). Every 'Fire' has 'Smoke'.
18.48.application: Don't 'Wait for Perfect'. Every job has 'Downsides'. If you 'Quit' accounting because it's 'Dry', and try art, you'll find art is 'Messy'. Don't chase 'Perfect Work'. Chase 'Perfect Focus' within your 'Natural Work'. Accept the 'Smoke' of life.

18.49.sankara: He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, who has subdued his self, from whom desire has fled, he by renunciation attains the supreme state of freedom from action.
18.49.meaning: The Goal: Naishkarmya-Siddhim. 1. Asakta-buddhih (Unattached mind). 2. Jita-atma (Self-mastered). 3. Vigata-sprihah (Desireless). This is 'Nish-karmya' (Action-less perfection).
18.49.application: Master the 'Interior'. Freedom isn't 'Not doing anything'. It's being so 'Unattached' and 'Masterful' that 'Doing' doesn't leave a 'Dint' on your soul. You reach the 'Action-less state' while 'Moving mountains'. Be 'Still' in the 'Storm'.

18.50.sankara: Learn from Me in brief, O Arjuna, how he who has attained perfection reaches Brahman, that supreme state of knowledge.
18.50.meaning: The Final Ascent. Krishna describes the steps from 'Action-less perfection' to 'Brahman' (The Absolute). The final 'Summary' of the path.
18.50.application: Follow the 'Checklist'. Krishna is giving the 'Final Brief' (Samasena). He will explain how to 'Solidify' your 'Siddhim' into 'Total Awareness'. Pay 'Intense Attention' to the next few steps.

18.51.sankara: Endowed with a pure intellect, controlling the self by firmness, relinquishing sound and other objects and abandoning love and hatred...
18.51.meaning: Step 1: The Setup. 1. Buddhya vishuddhaya (Pure Intellect). 2. Dhrt-yatmanam niyamya (Firmly self-controlled). 3. Tyaktva vishayan (Giving up sense objects). 4. Raga-dvesha vyudasya (Relinquishing Love/Hate).
18.51.application: Clean the 'Lens'. Before the 'Deep Dive' into Brahman, you must 'Calm the Senses'. Stop 'Chasing Sounds/Sights' and stop 'Hating/Loving' the environment. Become 'Neutral'. Pure 'Logic' (Buddhi) requires a 'Clean Heart'.

18.52.sankara: Dwelling in solitude, eating but little, with speech, body and mind subdued, always engaged in concentration and meditation, taking refuge in dispassion...
18.52.meaning: Step 2: The Practice. 1. Vivikta-sevi (Solitude). 2. Laghu-ashi (Light eating). 3. Yat-vak-kaya-manah (Subdued Speech/Body/Mind). 4. Dhyana-yoga-paro (Deep Meditation).
18.52.application: The 'Monk mindset'. To find the 'Infinite', you need 'Solitude'. Eat 'Little' so your energy isn't wasted on 'Digestion'. Keep the 'Machine' quiet. Spend more time in 'Dhyana' (Meditation) than in 'Debate'. Find the 'Vairagyam' (Dispassion).

18.53.sankara: Having abandoned egoism, force, arrogance, desire, anger and covetousness, free from the notion of 'mine' and peaceful, he is fit for becoming Brahman.
18.53.meaning: Step 3: The De-construction. Abandoning 1. Ahamkaram (Ego). 2. Balam (Force/Violence). 3. Darpam (Arrogance). 4. Kamam (Lust). 5. Krodham (Anger). 6. Parigraham (Possessiveness).
18.53.application: Become 'Transparent'. You are 'Fit for Brahman' (Brahma-bhuyaya kalpate) only when you 'Weight Nothing'. Drop the 'I-Mine' (Nirmamah) baggage. When there is 'No Ego' left, only 'Brahman' remains. Be 'Shantah' (Peaceful).

18.54.sankara: Becoming Brahman, serene in the Self, he neither grieves nor desires; the same to all beings, he obtains supreme devotion unto Me.
18.54.meaning: The State of Brahman. 'Brahma-bhutah' (Becoming Brahman). 1. Prasanna-atma (Cheerful Soul). 2. Na shochati (No grief). 3. Na kankshati (No desire). 4. Samah sarveshu (Equal to all).
18.54.application: Join the 'Infinite'. In this state, 'Grief/Desire' are impossible because you ARE everything. You have 'Highest Devotion' (Mad-bhaktim labhate param). You don't 'Worship' a goal; you 'Inhabit' the God.

18.55.sankara: By devotion he knows Me in truth, what and who I am; then having known Me in truth, he forthwith enters into the Supreme.
18.55.meaning: The Final Merger. Through 'Bhakti', he knows 'Yavan yash-chasmi' (Who and What I really am). Having 'Known' (Jnatva), he 'Enters' (Vishate) the Supreme.
18.55.application: The 'Great Disappearance'. You don't 'See' God; you 'Become' the Gaze. To 'Know Me' (The Divine) is to 'Merge' with Me. There is no 'Distance' left between the 'Lover' and the 'Beloved'. Total 'Union' (Vishate).

18.56.sankara: Doing all actions always, taking refuge in Me, by My grace he obtains the eternal, indestructible state or abode.
18.56.meaning: Grace in Action. Even if you are 'Doing all actions' (Sarva-karman-api), if you 'Take Refuge' (Mad-vyapashrayah) and have 'My Grace' (Mat-prasadad), you reach the 'Eternal' (Shashvatam).
18.56.application: The 'Safety Net'. You don't have to be a 'Perfect Monk'. If you just 'Put God in Charge' of your actions, 'HIS Grace' does the heavy lifting. You can be a 'CEO' or a 'Cabbie', and still reach the 'Eternal Abode' via 'Grace'.

18.57.sankara: Mentally resigning all actions to Me, regarding Me as the supreme goal, resorting to the Yoga of discrimination, do thou ever fix thy mind on Me.
18.57.meaning: The Metal Shift. 1. Chetasa (Mentally). 2. Sarva-karmani sanyasya (Resigning all acts). 3. Buddhi-yogam-upashritya (Resorting to Yoga of Intellect). 4. Mac-chittah (Mind on Me).
18.57.application: Run your life from 'The Cloud'. Keep your 'Physical Workspace' in the world, but your 'Mental Storage' in Me. Use your 'Intellect' (Buddhi-yoga) to keep the 'Link' active. Be 'Logged In' to God 24/7.

18.58.sankara: Fixing thy mind on Me, thou shalt, by My grace, overcome all obstacles; but if from egoism thou wilt not hear Me, thou shalt perish.
18.58.meaning: The Ultimatum. 1. 'Mac-chittah' -> 'Tarishyasi' (Cross all obstacles by Grace). 2. 'Ahamkarat' (From Ego) -> 'Vinakshyasi' (You will perish).
18.58.application: Listen or 'Lose'. This is the 'Final Warning'. If you try to 'Solve life' with your 'Small Ego', you will get 'Crushed' (Vinakshyasi). if you 'Fix your mind on Me', life's 'Problems' (Durgani) become 'Puddles' you can step over. 'Grace' is your 'Cheat Code'.

18.59.sankara: If, filled with egoism, thou thinkest 'I will not fight', vain is this, thy resolve; nature will compel thee.
18.59.meaning: The Compulsion of Nature. Arjuna says: 'I won't fight'. Krishna says: 'Lol, your Nature (Svabhava) will FORCE you'. You cannot run from your 'Coding'.
18.59.application: Surrender to your 'Program'. You can 'Pretend' to be a 'Saint', but if you are a 'Warrior' (or a Coder, or a Teacher) inside, your 'Nature' will eventually 'Throw you back into the game'. Stop fighting your 'Inherent Drive'. Use it for 'Good'.

18.60.sankara: O Arjuna, bound by thy own karma (action) born of thy own nature, that which from delusion thou wishest not to do, even that thou shalt do helplessly.
18.60.meaning: The Trap of Svabhava. 'Svabhava-jena karmana' (Born of nature). You are 'Free' to choose, but you are 'Bound' to your 'Tendencies'. You will do it 'Avasho' (Helplessly).
18.60.application: Accept the 'Inertia'. You are like a 'Trains on tracks'. You can choose the 'Speed', but the 'Tracks' (Your Nature) are already laid. Don't waste energy trying to 'Jump the tracks'. Just 'Drive the train' with 'Highest Awareness'.

18.61.sankara: The Lord dwells in the hearts of all beings, O Arjuna, causing all beings, by His illusive power, to revolve as if mounted on a machine.
18.61.meaning: The Divine Operator. 'Ishvarah sarva-bhutanam' (The Lord of all beings) lives in the 'Hrid-deshe' (Heart-region). He turns everyone like they are on a 'Yantra' (Machine/Carousel).
18.61.application: You are 'Riding', not 'Driving'. The 'Body-Mind' complex is a 'Bio-machine' (Yantra) operated by the 'Divine Intelligence' (Maya). Don't take it too seriously. Enjoy the 'Ride' while realizing who the 'Owner' is. You are the 'Guest' in the heart.

18.62.sankara: Fly unto Him for refuge with all thy being, O Arjuna; by His grace shalt thou obtain supreme peace and the eternal, abode.
18.62.meaning: The Exit Strategy. 'Tam-eva sharanam gachcha' (Go to HIM for refuge) with 'Sarva-bhavena' (Whole heart). Through 'Tat-prasadat' (His Grace), you get 'Param Shantim' (Supreme Peace).
18.62.application: Surrender is the 'Highest Logic'. When you realize you are on a 'Machine' you can't control, the smartest move is to 'Trust the Operator'. Don't hold back. Give 100% of your 'Bhav' (Feeling) to the Divine. Peace is a 'Gift', not a 'Goal'.

18.63.sankara: Thus has wisdom more secret than secrecy itself been declared to you by Me; having reflected over it fully, then act as thou wishest.
18.63.meaning: The Gift of Free Will. 'Guhyad guhyataram' (Secret of Secrets). Krishna has said it all. Now: 'Vimrish-yaitad asheshena' (Reflect on it fully) and 'Yathechchhasi tatha kuru' (Do as you wish).
18.63.application: Think for 'Yourself'. God doesn't 'Micromanage'. He gives you the 'Manual' (Gita) and then lets you 'Choose'. Real 'Freedom' comes after 'Deep Reflection'. Don't act out of 'Blind Faith'. Act out of 'Convinced Understanding'. It's your 'Call'.

18.64.sankara: Hear thou again My supreme word, most secret of all; because thou art dearly beloved of Me, therefore I will tell thee what is good for thee.
18.64.meaning: The Final Whisper. Krishna can't help himself—He loves Arjuna so much (Ishto-asi) that He wants to say the 'Supreme Word' (Paramam Vachah) one last time. For Arjuna's 'Hitam' (Benefit).
18.64.application: Listen to the 'Heart of God'. Beyond all 'Rules' and 'Logic', there is a 'Fatherly Love'. If you feel 'Beloved' by the Divine, you can hear the 'Final Truth' clearly. This is for your 'Good', not because God needs anything from you.

18.65.sankara: Fix thy mind on Me, be devoted to Me, sacrifice to Me, bow down to Me. Thou shalt come even to Me; truly do I promise unto thee, (for) thou art dear to Me.
18.65.meaning: The 4-Step Invitation. 1. Man-mana (Mind on Me). 2. Mad-bhakto (Devoted). 3. Mad-yaji (Sacrifice). 4. Namas-kuru (Bow). The result: 'Mam-eva-eshyasi' (You will reach Me).
18.65.application: Keep it 'Simple'. Fix your mind, give your heart, work for Him, and stay humble (Bow). This 'Promise' (Satyam te pratijane) is 'Unbreakable'. You are 'Dear' (Priyah) to the Universe. Just 'Walk Home'.

18.66.sankara: Abandoning all duties, take refuge in Me alone; I will liberate thee from all sins; grieve not.
18.66.meaning: The Ultimate Verse (Charama Shloka). 'Sarva-dharman parityajya' (Abandoning all 'Dharmas' or identities). 'Mam-ekam sharanam' (Refuge in Me alone). 'Ma shuchah' (Grieve not).
18.66.application: The 'Delete All' Key. Stop trying to be a 'Good Son', 'Good Warrior', or 'Good Monk'. Just be 'Mine'. When you drop all your 'Limited Roles' and 'Rules', I take over your 'Karma' (Mokshayishyami). 'Don't Worry'. You've arrived.

18.67.sankara: This is never to be spoken by thee to one who is devoid of austerities, nor to one who is not devoted, nor to one who does not render service, nor who speaks ill of Me.
18.67.meaning: Protecting the Seed. Don't share this with: 1. A-tapaskaya (No discipline). 2. A-bhaktaya (No devotion). 3. A-shushrushave (No service/desire to hear). 4. Abhisu-yati (Haters).
18.67.application: Be 'Selective' with Truth. Don't 'Cast Pearls'. High Wisdom requires a 'Prepared Soil'. If someone doesn't 'Respect' the message, the message will only 'Confuse' or 'Irritate' them. Keep the 'Flame' safe until the 'Lamp' is ready.

18.68.sankara: He who with supreme devotion to Me will teach this supreme secret to My devotees, shall doubtless come to Me.
18.68.meaning: The Reward for Sharing. Teaching this 'Paramam Guhyam' (Supreme Secret) to devotees with 'Para Bhakti' (Supreme Devotion).
18.68.application: Be a 'Light-Bearer'. Sharing 'Wisdom' is the 'Highest Service'. If you help others find their 'Internal God', you find your own 'External Path' cleared. You reach 'Me' (The Divine) by 'Reflecting' Me to others.

18.69.sankara: Nor is there any among men who does dearer service to Me than he; nor shall there be another on earth dearer to Me than he.
18.69.meaning: The Dearest Servant. 'Na-cha tasman' (None other than him). No human is 'More Dear' (Priya-krit-tamah) than the one who spreads this message.
18.69.application: The 'Favorite' Child. If you want to be 'Close' to the Divine, 'Serve' those who are 'Seeking' the Divine. The one who 'Clarifies' the Gita for others is the 'Dearest' in the whole world. 'Communication' is 'Sacrament'.

18.70.sankara: And he who will study this sacred dialogue of ours, by him I shall have been worshipped by the sacrifice of wisdom; such is My conviction.
18.70.meaning: The Sacrifice of Study. 'Adhyeshyate' (Studying) this 'Dharmyam Samvadam' (Sacred Dialogue) is 'Jnana-Yajnen' (Sacrifice of Knowledge).
18.70.application: Read to 'Worship'. Every time you 'Open the Gita' and try to 'Understand', you are performing a 'High Ritual'. 'Thinking' is a 'Sacrifice' when directed towards the 'Truth'. Your 'Brain-power' is a 'Holy Offering'.

18.71.sankara: The man also who hears this, full of faith and free from malice, he, too, liberated, shall attain to the happy worlds of those of righteous deeds.
18.71.meaning: The Power of Listening. Even just 'Shraddhavan' (With Faith) and 'Anasuyash-cha' (Without Malice) 'Listening' (Shrunuyad-api) is enough for liberation.
18.71.application: Just 'Be Present'. You don't have to be a 'Scholar'. If you listen with a 'Clean Heart', you get 'Liberated' (Muktah) and reach 'Subhan Lokan' (Happy Worlds). The 'Sound' of Truth is 'Medicine'.

18.72.sankara: Has this been heard, O Arjuna, with an attentive mind? Has the delusion of thy ignorance been destroyed, O Dhananjaya?
18.72.meaning: The Teacher's Check-in. Krishna asks: 1. 'Kachchit-shrutam' (Did you hear?). 2. 'Ekagrena chetasa' (With one-pointed mind?). 3. 'Moha-pranash-tah' (Is your delusion gone?).
18.72.application: Audit your 'Awareness'. Don't just 'Finish' the book. Ask yourself: 'Am I different now?' If the 'Ignorance-Delusion' (A-jnana-mohah) is still there, you weren't 'Attentive' (Ekagrena). Re-read until you 'Get it'.

18.73.sankara: Arjuna said: Destroyed is my delusion, as I have gained my memory (knowledge) through Thy grace, O Krishna. I am firm; my doubts are gone. I will do Thy word.
18.73.meaning: The Recovery of Self. 'Nashto mohah' (Delusion is destroyed). 'Smritir labdha' (Memory/Awareness regained) by 'Mat-prasadat' (Thy Grace). 'Gata-sandehah' (Doubts gone). 'Karishye vachanam tava' (I will do Thy word).
18.73.application: The 'Yes' of the Soul. This is the goal of all education: to 'Remember' who you are. When 'Memory' (Smriti) returns, 'Fear' leaves. You don't need to 'Think' anymore; you just 'Act' (Karishye). Say 'Yes' to your 'Highest Calling'.

18.74.sankara: Sanjaya said: Thus I have heard this wonderful dialogue between Krishna and the high-souled Arjuna, which causes the hair to stand on end.
18.74.meaning: The Witness's Response. Sanjaya is 'Electrified'. 'Romaharshanam'. This 'Samvadam' (Dialogue) is 'Adbhuta' (Wonderful).
18.74.application: Feel the 'Thrill'. If your 'Hair doesn't stand on end' when you realize the 'Infinite' is talking to you, you missed the 'Vibe'. Truth is not 'Boring'; it's 'Electrifying'. Be like Sanjaya—be a 'Channel' for the 'Wow'.

18.75.sankara: Through the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme and most secret Yoga direct from Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, Himself declaring it.
18.75.meaning: The Direct Link. Sanjaya heard it via 'Vyasa-prasadad' (The grace of the Sage). He heard it 'Sakshat' (Directly) from the 'Yogeshwarat' (Lord of Yoga).
18.75.application: Acknowledge the 'Lineage'. You didn't 'Think this up'. You 'Heard it' because a 'Chain of Masters' (Vyasa, etc.) kept the 'Signal' alive. Be 'Grateful' for the 'Broadcast'. You are 'Tuning In' to the 'Universal Frequency'.

18.76.sankara: O King, remembering again and again this wonderful and holy dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna, I rejoice again and again.
18.76.meaning: The Joy of Repetition. 'Samsmritya samsmritya' (Remembering again and again). 'Hrishiyami cha muhur-muhuh' (I rejoice every moment).
18.76.application: Loop the 'Light'. The Gita isn't a 'One-time read'. It's a 'Song' you 'Hum' forever. The more you 'Re-play' it in your head, the more 'Rejoicing' (Hrishiyami) you feel. 'Happiness' comes from 'Holy Obsession'.

18.77.sankara: And, remembering again and again, also that most wonderful form of Krishna, great is my wonder, O King; and I rejoice again and again.
18.77.meaning: The Vision remains. Sanjaya remembers the 'Rupam-ati-adbhutam' (Most wonderful form—the Cosmic Force). He is 'Vismayo me mahan' (Greatly wondered).
18.77.application: Stay in 'Awe'. Never lose the sense of 'Magic' (Vismaya). The 'Cosmic Form' of reality is always right there, hiding behind the 'Normal'. If you start seeing 'God' in 'Everything', you will 'Rejoice' every time you 'Open your Eyes'.

18.78.sankara: Wherever is Krishna, the Lord of Yoga, wherever is Arjuna, the archer, there are prosperity, victory, happiness and sound policy; this is my conviction.
18.78.meaning: The Final Equation. 'Yatra Yogeshwarah Krishno / Yatra Partho Dhanurdharah'. Krishna (Divine Vision) + Arjuna (Human Effort) = 'Shrih-Vijayo-Bhutir' (Prosperity, Victory, Happiness).
18.78.application: The 'Winning Formula'. You need both 'Grace' (Krishna) and 'Grind' (Arjuna). If you have 'Internal Peace' AND 'External Action', 'Success' and 'Joy' are 'Guaranteed' (Dhruva-nitir). Be the 'Archer' who listens to the 'Avatar'. The End.
